title: Rich, Resilient American Yeast and Gooey Paella
description: A unique and flavorful paella dish that combines the richness of yeast with gooey ingredients, featuring a pressure-cooked twist and a pescatarian-friendly approach, served in a molcajete.

ingredients.name: Yeast extract
ingredients.notes: Available at most health food stores or online.

ingredients.name: Cinnamon stick
ingredients.notes: Used for flavor, not for eating.

ingredients.name: Diced tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Canned or fresh, whichever is convenient.

instructions: Add a 1/4 cup of olive oil to a pressure cooker over medium heat.
Add the sliced chorizo sausage and cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes, garlic, smoked paprika, and yeast extract, and stir to combine.
Add the short grain rice, clams, mussels, and water to the pressure cooker, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the pressure cooker with a lid.
Cook for 10-12 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Stir in the Gruyère cheese until melted and gooey.
Remove the pressure cooker from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes.
Transfer the paella to a molcajete and garnish with a cinnamon stick.
